title: Add CSO/ZSO/ISO header inspection to Troubleshoot tab
Rework assets.js
description: Now that we are building an index.html artifact we can rework this so the actual blobs are on-disk individually and not baked into a JS file at the outset but later assembled into the artifact. This avoids Claude trying to read these giant blobs

title: Create README explaining the project
description: Write a README.md explaining: what this tool does (CSO/ZSO/ISO converter + PS1-to-EBOOT), how to use it (serve locally or GitHub Pages), supported formats, multi-disc support, how compression works, prior art references, and that everything runs client-side in the browser.

title: EBOOT assembler — orchestrate PBP construction from parts
description: Top-level orchestrator that assembles a complete EBOOT.PBP: 1) Build PARAM.SFO, 2) Gather icons/pics (user-provided or defaults), 3) Get DATA.PSP blob, 4) Calculate all PBP header offsets (pad DATA.PSP to 0x10000 alignment), 5) Build PSAR via PSISOIMG (single-disc) or PSTITLEIMG (multi-disc), 6) Concatenate everything, 7) Write final PBP header with correct offsets. Module: assembler.js

title: Static asset blobs — DATA.PSP ELF and default icons
description: The DATA.PSP section contains a small static MIPS ELF that is identical for all PS1 EBOOTs. Extract the base64-encoded blob from popstationr's data.h (datapspbody[], 19002 bytes). Also provide a minimal default ICON0.PNG (80x80 black). These are static binary blobs shipped as base64 constants. Module: assets.js

title: EBOOT Web Worker — off-thread PBP construction with progress
description: Web Worker that orchestrates the EBOOT build. Receives File objects (disc images) + metadata (title, disc ID, compression level, optional icon PNGs) via postMessage. Imports all modules (sfo.js, toc.js, psisoimg.js, pstitleimg.js, assembler.js, assets.js). Reports progress back to main thread. Transfers final PBP buffer with zero-copy. Module: eboot-worker.js

title: EBOOT UI — multi-file input, disc ID, title, compression options
description: Extend index.html with an EBOOT creation panel. Needs: 1) Multi-file drag/drop for disc images (up to 4 BIN/ISO files for multi-disc), 2) Game title text input, 3) Disc ID input (e.g. SCUS94163) with auto-detect from ISO if possible, 4) Compression level slider (0-9), 5) Optional icon upload, 6) Progress bar, 7) Download EBOOT.PBP. Add tab/section toggle between CSO/ZSO converter and EBOOT creator. Module: index.html updates
close_reason: Implemented - tabbed UI with CSO/ZSO converter and EBOOT builder panels

title: PSTITLEIMG wrapper — multi-disc PSAR support
description: For multi-disc games (e.g. FF7 = 4 discs), wrap multiple PSISOIMG sections in a PSTITLEIMG0000 container. PSTITLEIMG header at PSAR start, disc offsets at +0x200 (uint32 each, 0x00000000 = no more discs). First disc starts at +0x400. Each disc is a complete PSISOIMG section. User provides multiple BIN/ISO files (or a .m3u playlist). PARAM.SFO gets DISC_TOTAL=N. Module: pstitleimg.js

title: PSISOIMG writer — single-disc PSAR construction
description: Core conversion engine for a single PS1 disc. Writes PSISOIMG0000 header (12-byte magic + metadata), embeds TOC at +0x400, builds ISO index table (32 bytes per entry: offset u32 + length u32 + 24 bytes padding), compresses each 0x9300-byte ISO block with deflate (raw, wbits -15, configurable level 0-9). Falls back to uncompressed if compressed >= 0x9300. Writes STARTDAT header+footer after ISO data. Must run in Web Worker. Reports progress. Module: psisoimg.js

title: CUE parser — extract track layout from .cue sheets
description: Parse .cue file text to extract track layout: track number, type (MODE2/2352 for data, AUDIO for CDDA), pregap, index positions. Used by TOC generator to build accurate TOC for multi-track discs. Most PS1 games are single data track but some have CDDA audio tracks. Module: cue.js

title: Disc ID auto-detect — read SYSTEM.CNF from ISO filesystem
description: PS1 ISOs contain SYSTEM.CNF at a known location in the ISO9660 filesystem. Parse just enough ISO9660 (primary volume descriptor at sector 16, root directory record) to locate and read SYSTEM.CNF. Extract the BOOT line (e.g. BOOT=cdrom:\SCUS_941.63;1) and derive the disc ID (SCUS94163). This avoids requiring the user to manually enter it. Module: discid.js

title: PBP header writer — 0x28-byte header with section offsets
description: Write the PBP container header (40 bytes). Signature 00504250, version, and 8 uint32 offsets pointing to: PARAM.SFO, ICON0.PNG, ICON1.PMF, PIC0.PNG, PIC1.PNG, SND0.AT3, DATA.PSP, DATA.PSAR. Offsets are cumulative. Module: pbp.js

title: TOC generator — auto-generate CD table of contents from BIN/ISO
description: Generate a TOC (table of contents) for single-track PS1 data discs. Most PS1 games are single data track. TOC entries are 10-byte BCD-encoded structs (adr/control, tno, point, amin, asec, aframe, zero, pmin, psec, pframe). Need entries for: track 1 (data), lead-in (A0, A1, A2 points). Calculate from file size. Also support parsing .cue files for multi-track. Module: toc.js
close_reason: Implemented in toc.js with both auto-generate and CUE-based TOC

title: PARAM.SFO builder — binary key-value metadata
description: Build PARAM.SFO from scratch in JS. PSF magic (00505346), version 1.1, key table, data table, index entries. Required keys: BOOTABLE=1, CATEGORY=ME, DISC_ID (e.g. SCUS94163), DISC_VERSION=1.00, PARENTAL_LEVEL=0, PSP_SYSTEM_VER=3.01, REGION=0x8000, TITLE=game name. For multi-disc: DISC_TOTAL=N, DISC_NUMBER=1. Module: sfo.js
